contract.goal: Delete install.sh (now obsolete after the plugin-install flow lands) and add a grep tripwire test that asserts no remaining references to install.sh outside the project-substrate records (which intentionally preserve the migration's narrative).
acceptance_criteria: cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts (new) — grep tripwire reads every tracked file via git ls-files; asserts zero `install.sh` references outside `projects/` (substrate records) and the test file itself.

Non-substrate refs cleaned up: README.md historical reference rephrased to 'monolithic symlink-farm setup script'; docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md rewritten to drop the ~/.agents symlink/install.sh resolution and replace with the plugin-relative path explanation; .gitignore comment for /bin/ updated to drop the install.sh mention.
npm test green (post-W9 the suite is 610/610; +2 from the W9 tripwire's 2 assertions).
rules_applied: Substrate carve-out: `projects/` records are immutable historical narrative — the migration's checkins + PLAN + RESEARCH name install.sh as part of the story and that's correct. Test exempts them via path prefix.
Self-carve-out: the tripwire test names the forbidden string by definition. Excluded via exact-path match.
Grep tripwire flavor matches V9 (cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts) and V11 (cli/skill-bodies-call-bare-commands.test.ts) — static fixture + vitest assertions.
disqualifiers: No edits to project-substrate records (projects/) — they're immutable history.
No content edits to skill bodies (W4 already migrated them).
No changes to sync-shared.ts or the synced plugin trees (script + trees are unaffected by install.sh removal).
inputs: V9 / V11 test pattern reference (static-fixture grep tripwires)

actions: Surveyed install.sh references via grep -rln: README.md line 137 (historical 'Migrated from monolithic install.sh symlink-farm'), docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md lines 85-88 (consumer-machine ~/.agents symlink explanation), and the file install.sh itself.

Rewrote docs/AGENT-CONVENTIONS.md's docs-path-resolution section: dropped the ~/.agents/docs symlink explanation; replaced with plugin-relative-path explanation (per W4 idiom — plugin's docs/ ships inside the plugin tree).
Rephrased README.md historical reference: 'monolithic install.sh symlink-farm' → 'monolithic symlink-farm setup script'. Same meaning; doesn't name the deleted file.

Wrote cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts: lists tracked files via execSync('git ls-files'), reads each, greps for 'install.sh', collects offending file:line:context, asserts the collected list is empty. Carve-outs: projects/ (immutable substrate records); cli/no-install-sh-refs.test.ts (self); node_modules/ (defensive).

First test run failed: .gitignore line 1 still mentioned install.sh in a comment ('# Generated on every `./install.sh` run — kept out of the repo to keep'). Updated the comment to refer to 'the legacy setup-script symlink farm (deleted in W9)' without naming install.sh literally.
npm test (full suite): 610/610 passed (was 608; +2 from W9 tripwire).
Re-ran scripts/sync-shared.ts --check: ok (3 plugins synced). W9 didn't touch the synced trees; this is the sanity check that drift hasn't re-introduced itself.
corrections: First tripwire run caught .gitignore's comment still naming install.sh. The lesson: 'no remaining references' per PLAN means every actionable surface — including comments in config files. Updated to drop the name; substantive meaning preserved.

scope: Non-substrate install.sh references cleaned up (README, AGENT-CONVENTIONS, .gitignore)
changes_since_previous: W8 (1cbc00e) rewrote the README for the plugin-install flow. W9 closes the loop: deletes the now-obsolete install.sh script, cleans up the few remaining non-substrate references, and adds a grep tripwire so anyone re-introducing the reference later gets a loud test failure. Phase 1 is functionally complete after this commit; the follow-up PR can open.
notes_for_pr: install.sh deletion is the final piece of the migration. The script's role (symlink-farm + chicken-and-egg bin shim generation) is now replaced by the plugin-install flow + the per-plugin bin shims (W6).

Tripwire scope: only non-substrate files are checked. The project's own checkin records + PLAN + RESEARCH name install.sh as part of the migration narrative — that's correct historical context. The tripwire exempts `projects/` via path prefix.

Subtle catch: .gitignore's existing comment block named install.sh ('# Generated on every `./install.sh` run'). Updated to refer to 'the legacy setup-script symlink farm (deleted in W9)' without naming the file. Comments in config files count as references for the tripwire's purpose.

After W9, Phase 1 of the project is functionally complete. All 10 PLAN workstreams (W0-W9) shipped. Verification matrix: V1 ✓ (docs-based), V2 deferred, V3 implicit (W4 cleaned all skill-body refs), V4 ✓ (W0), V5 ✓ (W0), V6/V7 ✓ deferred to post-merge install smoke tests, V8 deferred, V9 ✓ (W1), V10 ✓ (W7), V11 ✓ (W4). W9 grep tripwire is one more defensive layer in the same flavor.

Follow-up PR: with W7+W8+W9 all committed on marketplace-portable-install.followup, the PR-open step is ready. Body should reference the part-1 (PR #11) merge commit ef4c186 and call out per-plugin/cli/ as the largest single piece of the diff (W7's sync output, ~452 KB).
